refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall achieve and maintain operational availability of ≥0.97 (97%) over the agreed evaluation period, measured as the ratio of time the system is available for productive operation to total scheduled operational time, excluding planned maintenance windows.
refined_fr.rationale: CR-NGHE-004 is a key performance indicator (KPI) that directly impacts customer satisfaction and mission success. Refinement clarifies that availability is a measurable, time-based metric with explicit exclusions (planned maintenance) and a defined evaluation period. This transforms a vague 'meet target' statement into a verifiable requirement with clear boundary conditions.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Continuous telemetry logging of system state (operational, degraded, failed, maintenance) with automated availability calculation: Availability = (Total Scheduled Time - Unplanned Downtime) / Total Scheduled Time. Validation against customer-agreed baseline over minimum 30-day evaluation window.
sub_frs: FR-NGHE-004.1: The NGHE solution shall detect and report system faults within 5 seconds of occurrence.
FR-NGHE-004.2: The NGHE solution shall enable rapid fault diagnosis and corrective action to minimize mean time to repair (MTTR).
FR-NGHE-004.3: The NGHE solution shall provide predictive maintenance alerts to prevent unplanned failures.
FR-NGHE-004.4: The NGHE solution shall maintain redundancy in critical subsystems to tolerate single-point failures.

candidate_dps: DP-A: Implement comprehensive health monitoring across all subsystems with real-time fault detection, automated diagnostics, and predictive maintenance scheduling integrated with a centralized availability tracking dashboard.
DP-B: Deploy redundant critical components (dual motors, dual control channels, dual communication links) with automatic failover logic and continuous self-test protocols.
DP-C: Establish a tiered maintenance strategy combining condition-based predictive maintenance (via sensor analytics), preventive maintenance scheduling, and rapid on-site spare parts logistics.
DP-D: Integrate all three approaches (DP-A + DP-B + DP-C) as a holistic availability assurance system with cross-subsystem health correlation and adaptive maintenance triggers.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP-D: Integrate comprehensive health monitoring (real-time fault detection, diagnostics, predictive alerts), redundancy in critical subsystems (dual motors, dual control channels, automatic failover), and tiered maintenance strategy (condition-based + preventive + logistics) as a unified availability assurance system with cross-subsystem health correlation and adaptive maintenance triggers.

potential_sources: Health monitoring system must interface with Vehicle subsystems (motor, hydraulics, electrical) and Teleoperation control channels—moderate coupling, but well-defined via Telemetry interface.
Redundancy logic (failover) couples Vehicle architecture to Safety Services for safe state transitions—acceptable coupling, managed via SafetyAlert interface.
Predictive maintenance scheduling couples to maintenance planning workflows outside NGHE solution scope—low coupling, managed via external data export.
coupling_check.notes: DP-D is comprehensive but not over-coupled. Each sub-strategy (monitoring, redundancy, maintenance) has clear ownership and interface boundaries. Coupling is primarily through well-defined telemetry and safety alert channels, which are already planned interfaces.

assumptions_risks: ASSUMPTION: Sensor reliability and calibration are sufficient to enable accurate health monitoring; if sensors drift or fail, fault detection accuracy degrades.
ASSUMPTION: Redundant components (dual motors, dual channels) are available within cost and weight constraints; if not, availability target may be unachievable.
ASSUMPTION: Predictive maintenance algorithms (e.g., remaining useful life models) are validated and tuned for NGHE operating conditions; if not, false positives/negatives will reduce effectiveness.
RISK: Failover logic complexity may introduce latency or race conditions; inadequate testing could cause unsafe transitions.
RISK: Spare parts logistics and on-site availability are customer-controlled; NGHE cannot guarantee MTTR if spares are unavailable.
RISK: High safety criticality (per tailoring factors) means any availability improvement must not compromise safety; redundancy and failover must be fail-safe.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: DP-D is sound but should be decomposed into explicit sub-DPs for each availability lever (monitoring, redundancy, maintenance) at the next level to clarify ownership and reduce implementation risk. Additionally, add explicit safety constraints: 'All redundancy and failover logic shall maintain or improve safety posture; no single failure shall result in unsafe state.'

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: The NGHE Solution module is the top-level system boundary that integrates Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services subsystems. It owns the availability assurance strategy, including health monitoring orchestration, redundancy coordination, and maintenance scheduling logic. The module is responsible for end-to-end availability measurement and reporting.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Bidirectional flow of health data (sensor readings, fault codes, system state) from Vehicle and Teleoperation subsystems to NGHE Solution availability monitoring engine, and export of availability metrics and predictive alerts back to external systems (customer dashboard, maintenance planning tools).

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: Outbound safety-critical alerts from NGHE Solution availability logic to Safety Services when redundancy is degraded, failover is triggered, or a fault threatens safe operation. Ensures safety manager is informed of availability-impacting events.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Inbound operational tasking and scheduling from Site Manager/Operator. NGHE Solution uses task schedule to define 'scheduled operational time' for availability calculation and to coordinate maintenance windows.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Communication backbone for telemetry, tasking, and safety alerts. NGHE Solution depends on Network Link availability; redundancy in Network Link is critical to overall system availability.

module_containment_scores.notes: NGHE Solution is the natural top-level owner of availability assurance. All monitoring, redundancy coordination, and maintenance logic are system-level concerns. Minimal diffusion: only external interfaces (Telemetry, SafetyAlert, Tasking, Network Link) cross the boundary. High containment because availability is a system-level KPI, not a subsystem concern.

Vehicle owns redundant hardware (dual motors, dual control channels) and local health sensors, but availability orchestration (failover logic, cross-subsystem health correlation, predictive maintenance scheduling) must reside at NGHE Solution level. If availability logic is distributed into Vehicle, it will diffuse across Vehicle and Teleoperation, creating coupling and maintenance burden.

Teleoperation owns redundant control channels and local diagnostics, but availability orchestration is a system-level concern. Assigning availability logic to Teleoperation would create tight coupling with Vehicle and Safety Services, violating separation of concerns.

Safety Services is responsible for safe state transitions and safety-critical alerts, not availability assurance. Assigning availability logic to Safety Services would conflate safety and availability concerns, increasing complexity and risk. Safety Services should consume SafetyAlert from NGHE Solution, not own availability logic.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: NGHE Solution is the optimal IM boundary for availability assurance because: (1) Availability is a system-level KPI that requires cross-subsystem visibility and coordination; (2) Health monitoring, redundancy orchestration, and maintenance scheduling are system-level concerns that should not be distributed across Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services; (3) Centralizing availability logic in NGHE Solution minimizes diffusion and coupling, enabling clear ownership and testability; (4) NGHE Solution already owns the top-level interfaces (Telemetry, Tasking, SafetyAlert, Network Link) needed to implement availability assurance.
containment_risks: RISK: If NGHE Solution availability logic becomes too complex or monolithic, it may be difficult to test and maintain. Mitigation: Decompose into sub-modules (Health Monitor, Redundancy Manager, Maintenance Scheduler) at the next level, each with clear ownership and interfaces.

RISK: If Vehicle or Teleoperation subsystems implement local availability logic (e.g., local failover), it may conflict with NGHE Solution-level orchestration, causing unsafe or inefficient behavior. Mitigation: Establish clear architectural rule that all availability-critical logic resides in NGHE Solution; Vehicle and Teleoperation implement only local health sensors and redundant hardware, not orchestration logic.

RISK: If Network Link fails, NGHE Solution cannot receive telemetry or send SafetyAlert, causing availability assurance to fail. Mitigation: Implement Network Link redundancy (dual communication channels) and local failover logic in Vehicle/Teleoperation that operates independently if Network Link is lost.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Assign IM boundary to NGHE Solution with explicit sub-module decomposition at the next level: (1) Health Monitor module (owns sensor aggregation, fault detection, diagnostics), (2) Redundancy Manager module (owns failover logic, redundancy state tracking), (3) Maintenance Scheduler module (owns predictive maintenance algorithms, scheduling). Each sub-module should have clear ownership, interfaces, and test strategy. Additionally, establish architectural rule that Vehicle and Teleoperation implement only local health sensors and redundant hardware, not system-level availability orchestration.

fr_dp_gate.reason: DP-D (integrated availability assurance system) directly addresses FR-NGHE-004 (achieve ≥0.97 availability). The DP covers all three availability levers: (1) health monitoring enables rapid fault detection and diagnosis (reduces MTTR), (2) redundancy enables failover and fault tolerance (reduces unplanned downtime), (3) predictive maintenance prevents failures (reduces unplanned downtime). The DP is implementable and measurable. No decoupling or independence issues detected.

diffusion_risks: DIFFUSION RISK: If health monitoring logic is distributed across Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services instead of centralized in NGHE Solution, it will create tight coupling and maintenance burden. Mitigation: Enforce architectural rule that all system-level availability logic resides in NGHE Solution.

DIFFUSION RISK: If redundancy failover logic is implemented in both Vehicle and NGHE Solution, it may cause race conditions or conflicting decisions. Mitigation: Assign failover orchestration to NGHE Solution; Vehicle implements only local redundant hardware and health sensors.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: NGHE Solution is the optimal IM boundary for availability assurance. Containment score of 95 indicates minimal diffusion. All external interfaces (Telemetry, SafetyAlert, Tasking, Network Link) are well-defined and already planned. No new interfaces required. Ownership is clear: NGHE Solution owns system-level availability logic; Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services own subsystem-level components and local diagnostics.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification of FR-NGHE-004 is feasible via continuous telemetry logging and automated availability calculation. NGHE Solution module will implement availability tracking dashboard that logs system state (operational, degraded, failed, maintenance) and calculates availability = (Total Scheduled Time - Unplanned Downtime) / Total Scheduled Time. Validation against customer-agreed baseline over minimum 30-day evaluation window. Telemetry interface provides necessary data flow.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Allocation is clear: NGHE Solution owns end-to-end availability assurance, including health monitoring, redundancy orchestration, and maintenance scheduling. Vehicle owns redundant hardware and local health sensors. Teleoperation owns redundant control channels and local diagnostics. Safety Services owns safe state transitions and safety-critical alerts. Interfaces are well-defined (Telemetry, SafetyAlert, Tasking, Network Link). No ambiguity in ownership or responsibility.

recommended_revisions: Decompose DP-D into explicit sub-DPs at the next level: (1) DP-D.1 Health Monitoring (sensor aggregation, fault detection, diagnostics), (2) DP-D.2 Redundancy Management (failover logic, redundancy state tracking), (3) DP-D.3 Maintenance Scheduling (predictive maintenance algorithms, scheduling). This will clarify ownership and reduce implementation risk.

Add explicit safety constraint to DP-D: 'All redundancy and failover logic shall maintain or improve safety posture; no single failure shall result in unsafe state.' This ensures availability improvements do not compromise safety (high safety criticality per tailoring factors).

Establish architectural rule: 'All system-level availability logic resides in NGHE Solution; Vehicle and Teleoperation implement only local health sensors and redundant hardware, not orchestration logic.' This prevents diffusion and coupling.

Define Network Link redundancy strategy: 'NGHE Solution shall implement dual communication channels (primary + backup) with automatic failover. Vehicle shall implement local failover logic that operates independently if Network Link is lost.' This ensures availability assurance is resilient to Network Link failures.

decision.justification: FR-NGHE-004 is a system-level KPI that requires decomposition into sub-FRs to clarify the specific availability levers (fault detection, redundancy, maintenance). The refined FR and selected DP-D are sound, but DP-D is too broad for direct implementation. Decomposition into sub-FRs (FR-NGHE-004.1 through FR-NGHE-004.4) will enable clearer DP selection and IM assignment at the next level. NGHE Solution is the correct IM boundary, but sub-module decomposition (Health Monitor, Redundancy Manager, Maintenance Scheduler) is needed to reduce complexity and clarify ownership.
decision.justification_detail: The refined FR is atomic in the sense that it defines a single, measurable KPI (availability ≥0.97). However, the DP-D is composite, covering three distinct availability levers (monitoring, redundancy, maintenance). To avoid over-coupling and diffusion, each lever should be decomposed into a separate sub-FR and sub-DP at the next level. This will enable clearer ownership, testability, and risk management.
next_level_sub_frs: FR-NGHE-004.1: The NGHE solution shall detect and report system faults within 5 seconds of occurrence to enable rapid diagnosis and corrective action.

FR-NGHE-004.2: The NGHE solution shall enable automatic failover to redundant subsystems (motors, control channels, communication links) to tolerate single-point failures and minimize unplanned downtime.
FR-NGHE-004.3: The NGHE solution shall provide predictive maintenance alerts based on sensor analytics and remaining useful life models to prevent unplanned failures.
FR-NGHE-004.4: The NGHE solution shall coordinate maintenance scheduling with operational tasking to minimize planned downtime and optimize availability.

assumptions: ASSUMPTION: Sensor reliability and calibration are sufficient to enable accurate health monitoring; if sensors drift or fail, fault detection accuracy degrades.
ASSUMPTION: Redundant components (dual motors, dual channels) are available within cost and weight constraints; if not, availability target may be unachievable.
ASSUMPTION: Predictive maintenance algorithms (e.g., remaining useful life models) are validated and tuned for NGHE operating conditions; if not, false positives/negatives will reduce effectiveness.
ASSUMPTION: Spare parts logistics and on-site availability are customer-controlled; NGHE cannot guarantee MTTR if spares are unavailable.
ASSUMPTION: Network Link redundancy is implemented (dual communication channels); if not, availability assurance is vulnerable to single-point failure.
ASSUMPTION: Vehicle and Teleoperation subsystems implement local health sensors and redundant hardware; NGHE Solution owns system-level orchestration logic.
open_risks: RISK: Failover logic complexity may introduce latency or race conditions; inadequate testing could cause unsafe transitions. MITIGATION: Implement formal verification of failover state machine; conduct extensive testing with fault injection.

RISK: High safety criticality (per tailoring factors) means any availability improvement must not compromise safety; redundancy and failover must be fail-safe. MITIGATION: Add explicit safety constraint to DP-D; conduct FMEA on redundancy and failover logic.

RISK: If health monitoring logic is distributed across Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services, it will create tight coupling and maintenance burden. MITIGATION: Enforce architectural rule that all system-level availability logic resides in NGHE Solution.

RISK: If Network Link fails, NGHE Solution cannot receive telemetry or send SafetyAlert, causing availability assurance to fail. MITIGATION: Implement Network Link redundancy (dual communication channels) and local failover logic in Vehicle/Teleoperation that operates independently if Network Link is lost.

RISK: Availability target (≥0.97) may be unachievable if MTTR is too high or failure rate is too high. MITIGATION: Conduct reliability analysis (FMEA, FTA) to identify critical failure modes and validate that DP-D can achieve target.
evidence_needed: EVIDENCE: Sensor specifications and calibration procedures to validate health monitoring accuracy.
EVIDENCE: Redundant component specifications (dual motors, dual control channels, dual communication links) and cost/weight analysis.
EVIDENCE: Predictive maintenance algorithm validation data (remaining useful life models, false positive/negative rates) for NGHE operating conditions.
EVIDENCE: Failover logic design and formal verification results (state machine correctness, latency analysis).
EVIDENCE: Safety analysis (FMEA, FTA) on redundancy and failover logic to ensure fail-safe behavior.
EVIDENCE: Network Link redundancy design and failover testing results.
EVIDENCE: Reliability analysis (FMEA, FTA) to identify critical failure modes and validate that DP-D can achieve ≥0.97 availability target.
EVIDENCE: Customer-agreed evaluation period and baseline availability metrics for validation.
